title: "Delight Becomes Pictorial"
content:
Delight becomes pictorial
    When viewed through pain, --
    More fair, because impossible
    That any gain.

    The mountain at a given distance
    In amber lies;
    Approached, the amber flits a little, --
    And that 's the skies!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled ""Delight Becomes Pictorial"", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
